resolvedQuestions: For each graduate: what does the implementation change on the production path?
For each graduate: untested scenario classes?
Where could each be a no-op?
What retrieval/graph principles predict scope beyond the benchmark?
keyFindings: Three of five graduated flags carry unresolved production gaps (bitemporal unwired, degree cap undefined, heartbeat cadence undefined)
All benchmarks ran against synthetic/read-only data, never live mutating production state
Conditional graduations dressed as unqualified verdicts across multiple clusters
Multihop is domain-specific heuristic (NNN-slug convention), not general retrieval
RRF benefit is marginal on calibrated lanes (37/42 agreement, 0.9762)
Dedup precision 1.0 on 17 synthetic records does not guarantee scale behavior
Citation ledger gated on data accumulation, not code correctness
Plain RRF regresses on conflict band (4/5 vs baseline 5/5) — conflict seam is required, not optional
ruledOutDirections: Re-testing CUT flags (5 flags already have valid cut evidence)
Breaking byte-identical OFF paths (all confirmed byte-identical when flags are OFF)
